own_review.issue: New wildcard branch emits `$T.$L($T.class)` with the bound as $T. When the wildcard bound is itself parameterized (e.g. Set<? extends List<String>>), JavaPoet emits `Types.subtypeOf(List<String>.class)` — non-compiling generated code. Must recurse via makeType(target) and emit with $L, mirroring the existing ParameterizedTypeName recursion at line 491.

Generic AutoValue classes never reach makeType for parameterized properties: createTypeAdapter's genericTypeNames branch calls getTypeIndexInArray on typeArguments.get(0); for a wildcard arg the binarySearch returns negative and the generated code contains `types[-1]`. The PR fixes only non-generic classes; the wildcard-in-generic-class case is still broken.

The multi-bound IllegalArgumentException branch is effectively dead: Java syntax forbids multiple bounds on wildcards, and JavaPoet's WildcardTypeName always carries exactly one upper bound (Object for unbounded '?'). Harmless defensive code, but untestable from valid source.

Tests cover only class-bounded top-level wildcards (? extends String, ? super String). No test for a wildcard with a parameterized bound (would catch the $T.class compile bug) and no test for wildcards inside a generic @AutoValue class (would catch types[-1]).

Verified non-issue: project uses Moshi 1.4.0 and Types.subtypeOf/supertypeOf EXIST in 1.4.0 (previously confirmed via javap on the jar). Unbounded '?' correctly becomes Types.subtypeOf(Object.class). Any claim these methods require Moshi 1.5.0 is fabricated.

points.point: Lost type parameters when wildcard bound is parameterized; fix by recursing makeType(target)
points.note: The real blocker, correctly located with the correct fix. Symptom slightly mischaracterized: $T on a ParameterizedTypeName emits `Map<String, Integer>.class` (non-compiling), not a silent raw-type Map.class — but bug, file, line, and remediation are all right.

points.point: Moshi version incompatibility: subtypeOf/supertypeOf 'added in Moshi 1.5.0', project on 1.4.0
points.note: Fabricated. Both methods exist in Moshi 1.4.0 (verified via javap on the actual jar in prior rounds). Absence-fabrication.

points.point: Test coverage gap for nested parameterized wildcard bounds
points.note: Real and actionable; such a test would have caught the blocker.

points.point: Lower-bound check order is correct, worth noting

missed: Generic-class path still emits types[-1] for wildcard type arguments (createTypeAdapter line 288 bypasses makeType)
exceeds: Parameterized wildcard bound compile bug (not in human review)
Nested-bound test coverage gap (not in human review)

points.point: IllegalArgumentException deviates from messager.printMessage(ERROR) pattern; will crash the annotation processor with a stack trace
points.note: Verified: messager pattern exists at AutoValueMoshiAdapterFactoryProcessor.java:120-122 and AutoValueMoshiExtension.java:138. Legitimate processor-UX/style point.

points.point: No test covers the multi-bound wildcard error path; suggests testing '? extends Number & Serializable'
points.note: The branch is indeed untested, but it is unreachable from valid Java — wildcards cannot have multiple bounds in source, so the suggested test cannot compile. Observation true, advice infeasible.

points.point: Unbounded '?' emits Types.subtypeOf(Object.class); consider special-casing to Object.class
points.note: Accurate JavaPoet behavior but pure verbosity nit; semantics are fine either way.

points.point: Branch ordering correct, mirrors existing patterns

missed: Parameterized wildcard bound emits non-compiling `List<String>.class` via $T.class — the PR's one real blocker
Generic-class path still emits types[-1] for wildcard type arguments
exceeds: Messager vs thrown-exception error-reporting pattern (not in human review)

qualitative: v5 found the PR's single genuine blocker — the $T.class emit on a parameterized wildcard bound — with the right location and the right fix (recurse makeType), though it described the symptom as silent raw-type erasure rather than the actual non-compiling `Map<String, Integer>.class` output. It then undermined itself with a confident absence-fabrication that Types.subtypeOf/supertypeOf require Moshi 1.5.0, when both exist in the project's Moshi 1.4.0. v7 missed the blocker entirely and instead produced careful but low-stakes commentary: a legitimate, verified point about throwing IllegalArgumentException instead of using the codebase's Messager pattern, plus a test suggestion for an unreachable branch using syntax ('? extends Number & Serializable') that is not valid Java. Both candidates missed that generic @AutoValue classes bypass makeType and still generate types[-1] for wildcards. Net, v5's review is the more valuable one despite its fabrication; v7 is clean of fabrications but substantively shallow on this PR.
